clique-descent.last_note: Reframed off false Lemma S: self-blocking clutter + finite-ground-set Prop 2 + mutual-witness Lemma 3 reduce crux to Q finite (finite fibers of Phi); that fiber bound still open.
clique-descent.summary: Descending chain of admissible sets stabilizes via essential-prime finiteness (p<=maxpf(a1)); stabilized set is a GCD-clique periodic union of residue classes; sequence is its greedy enumeration from n=1. Proved tools: gaps<=a1, pairwise gcd>1.

descent-shared-prime.last_note: Lemma S-prime proved by minimum-height violating-pair descent (companion x, a1<=x<a_j); Prop 1-prime + certified finish close the whole problem. SOLVED.
descent-shared-prime.summary: Minimal-counterexample descent proving Lemma S-prime (any two clauses share a prime <= a1); clean Prop 1-prime reduction makes finite ground set {primes<=a1}; sole gap = q-cofactor descent closing.

finite-state-bijection.summary: Finite-state machine: residues mod M in admissible set A; successor is a cyclic bijection (reversible) giving PURE periodicity from n=1. Headlines the for-every-n upgrade.

sieve-closure.last_note: Complete modulo one crux (essential primes finite); closure fixed-point + density monovariant + full for-every-n finish proved; monovariants do not close the gap.
sieve-closure.summary: Fixed-point/closure: build the self-consistent (GCD-clique) periodic set directly; vacuous-constraint lemma fixes it; finiteness via coprime-pair monovariant. Same clean for-all-n finish.
